msg: a1-reconcile fixture suite writes live timestamps into checked-in fixture files (single-missing/single-pass drift-*.md) instead of a mktemp copy, violating CONVENTIONS.md Isolation section; reverted the resulting diff post-run, did not fix the suite itself (out of Wave 1 scope).

Mandatory const sweep found SQL_TYPE_ALIASES, a module-level const consumed only by normalizeSqlType, not named on Wave 2 MOVE list (only SQL_COLDEF_STOPWORDS was flagged); moved it too since leaving it in facade would ReferenceError.

Mandatory const sweep found REALPATH_DEFAULT_REAL_MARKERS, a module-level const consumed only by cmdRealpathCheckRun via direct reference (new RegExp(flags[...] || REALPATH_DEFAULT_REAL_MARKERS)), not named anywhere in Wave 4 MOVE list text (plan named REALPATH_MOCK_MARKERS/URL/LOCALHOST plus flagged REALPATH_SIGNATURES for re-check, but missed this fifth constant entirely); moved it alongside the other four since leaving it in the facade would ReferenceError on first realpath-check run invocation.

Plan Task 6.1 Step 2 assumed pack.cjs reuses io.cjs parseFrontmatter; reading the full moved block showed parsePackYaml/parsePatternFile only reuse the frontmatter grammar conceptually via their own from-scratch parser, never call parseFrontmatter itself. Imported the deps the code actually calls instead (parseFlags, vaultRoot from io.cjs) rather than shipping a dead parseFrontmatter import.

worktree.cjs does not import io.cjs fail() at all — cmdWorktreePrepare shadows it with a local const fail(name,hint) validation-check accumulator; traced every call site before deciding not to import the shared helper.

plan Step 4 suggested importing prReviewDir/ensurePrReviewDir into pr.cjs from worktree-registry.cjs, but grep confirmed neither is actually called anywhere in the moved pr functions (only referenced in a stale facade comment) — omitted both to avoid a dead import, same over-specification pattern as Wave 6 io.cjs parseFrontmatter finding.

Wave 11 const-sweep found SPEC_STATUS_TO_PHASE, a module-level lookup object not named in the plan MOVE list, consumed via bracket lookup (not .has/.match/.test) inside cmdSpecUpdateStatus; moved it alongside the four named functions to avoid a ReferenceError strand, same detection-blind-spot class as Wave 2 SQL_TYPE_ALIASES.

Const-sweep found ANALYSIS_STATUS_TO_PHASE, an undocumented module-level phase-lookup object between cmdAnalyzeInit and cmdAnalyzeUpdateStatus, consumed via bracket lookup inside cmdAnalyzeUpdateStatus; not named in plan Wave 12 MOVE list (same detection-blind-spot class as Wave 11's SPEC_STATUS_TO_PHASE and Wave 2's SQL_TYPE_ALIASES). Moved alongside the named functions to avoid a ReferenceError.

Const-sweep found undocumented MODERNIZE_STATUS_TO_PHASE bracket-lookup object (same pattern class as Wave 11 SPEC_STATUS_TO_PHASE and Wave 12 ANALYSIS_STATUS_TO_PHASE), moved alongside the named functions to avoid stranding cmdModernizeUpdateStatus with a ReferenceError.

Const-sweep found undocumented RECONCILE_STATUS_TO_PHASE bracket-lookup object (same pattern class as Wave 11 SPEC_STATUS_TO_PHASE, Wave 12 ANALYSIS_STATUS_TO_PHASE, Wave 13 MODERNIZE_STATUS_TO_PHASE), consumed via bracket lookup inside cmdReconcileUpdateStatus; not named in plan Wave 14 MOVE list. Moved alongside the named functions to avoid stranding cmdReconcileUpdateStatus with a ReferenceError. gitLastTouchIso byte-diff confirmed IDENTICAL pre/post move; a1-cmd-injection explicit re-run 7/7 green.

Const-sweep for Task 16.1 (fix) found no undocumented module-level consts in range — the fix group text in the plan already listed all its identifiers correctly (only function-local PHASE_MAP inside cmdFixUpdateStatus, not module-level, correctly not on the MOVE list).

Const-sweep for Task 16.2 (constitution) found TWO undocumented module-level consts not named in plan Wave 16 MOVE list: CONSTITUTION_STATUS_TO_PHASE (bracket-lookup phase-map object, same pattern class as Waves 11/12/13/14 STATUS_TO_PHASE findings, consumed inside cmdConstitutionUpdateStatus) and CLAUDEMD_LINK_MARKER_START/CLAUDEMD_LINK_MARKER_END (two string consts used by cmdConstitutionLinkClaudemd for idempotent marker-block detection). Both moved alongside the named functions to avoid stranding cmdConstitutionUpdateStatus/cmdConstitutionLinkClaudemd with a ReferenceError; no external callers outside the constitution range for either.
